display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Prompts (Batch Size B=2)
e.g., ['red', 'blue']
Why: Define inputs to compare

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Tokenize
Shape: [2, 8]
Why: Convert text to model input IDs

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Model Forward Pass
(Cache Layer 6 resid_post_mlp)
Why: Run model & capture internal state

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Cached Activations
Shape: [2, 8, 768]
Why: Get model's representation at Layer 6

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Process Activations
(Average over target span [2, 3))
Why: Get single vector for target word

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Target Activations
Shape: [2, 768]
Why: Input for comparing target representations

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Compute Jacobian (Blocks.6.MLP)
(Run forward/backward per prompt
Target Index: [2, 2])
Why: Find how params affect target output

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Jacobian Matrix J
Shape: [2, 4722432]
Why: Gradient vector per input

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Compute NTK = J @ J.T
Why: Measure similarity of gradients

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
NTK Matrix
Shape: [2, 2]
Why: Quantify functional similarity

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SAE Encode
Why: Project into sparse feature space

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Latent Features
Shape: [2, 32768]
Why: Sparse representation of target

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Latent (Norm=False)
Shape: [2, 32768]
Why: Keep original feature magnitudes

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
Latent (Norm=True)
(L2 Normalized)
Shape: [2, 32768]
Why: Focus only on feature pattern/direction

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SAE Sim ('raw')
latent @ latent.T
Why: Similarity based on angle & magnitude

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SAE Sim ('cosine')
(Internal L2 Norm)
norm_latent @ norm_latent.T
Why: Similarity based only on angle/direction

div.style: display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table-cell; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center;
display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SAE Sim ('raw')
(Dot Product on Normed)
latent @ latent.T
Why: Same as Cosine Sim if already normed

div.style: display: table; white-space: break-spaces; line-height: 1.5; max-width: 200px; text-align: center; width: 200px;

span.p:
SAE Sim ('cosine')
(Internal L2 Norm)
norm_latent @ norm_latent.T
Why: Similarity based only on angle/direction
